stemHtml: When carrying out an ILS autoland approach, if a glideslope signal failure occurs at 80 ft, which is below the Alert Height, what is the correct course of action action?

options.a: Revert to a visual landing using the localizer only.
options.b: Continue autoland, as 80 ft is too low for a failure to affect the landing.
options.d: A warning will trigger and a go-around must be initiated.
